```{python}
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    x = residuals.dropna().astype(float)
    # The first few state-space residuals are often diffuse-initialization artifacts.
    if len(x) > 24:
        x = x.iloc[12:]
    jb = stats.jarque_bera(x)
    shapiro_stat, shapiro_p = stats.shapiro(x) if len(x) <= 5000 else (np.nan, np.nan)
    return {
        "target": target,
        "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
        "n_residuals": int(len(x)),
        "mean_residual": float(x.mean()),
        "sd_residual": float(x.std(ddof=1)),
        "skewness": float(stats.skew(x, bias=False)),
        "excess_kurtosis": float(stats.kurtosis(x, fisher=True, bias=False)),
        "jarque_bera_statistic": float(jb.statistic),
        "jarque_bera_pvalue": float(jb.pvalue),
        "shapiro_wilk_statistic": float(shapiro_stat),
        "shapiro_wilk_pvalue": float(shapiro_p),
    }


def plot_residual_diagnostics(residuals: pd.Series, target: str, train_end: pd.Timestamp) -> None:
    """Save residual time-series, histogram, Q-Q, and ACF diagnostic plots.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    x = residuals.dropna().astype(float)
    if len(x) > 24:
        x = x.iloc[12:]
    target_label = "statewide overdose rate" if target == "statewide_rate" else "maximum H1 persistence"
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(11, 7.5))
    axes[0, 0].plot(x.index, x.values, color="#264653", lw=1.2)
    axes[0, 0].axhline(0, color="#333333", lw=0.8)
    axes[0, 0].set_title("Residuals over time")
    axes[0, 0].set_xlabel("Month")
    axes[0, 0].set_ylabel("Residual")

    axes[0, 1].hist(x.values, bins=24, density=True, alpha=0.55, color="#457b9d", edgecolor="white")
    grid = np.linspace(float(x.min()), float(x.max()), 200)
    if len(np.unique(x.values)) > 3:
        kde = stats.gaussian_kde(x.values)
        axes[0, 1].plot(grid, kde(grid), color="#a23b72", lw=1.8, label="kernel density")
    normal_pdf = stats.norm.pdf(grid, loc=float(x.mean()), scale=float(x.std(ddof=1)))
    axes[0, 1].plot(grid, normal_pdf, color="#111111", lw=1.2, ls="--", label="normal fit")
    axes[0, 1].set_title("Histogram and density")
    axes[0, 1].legend(fontsize=8)

    stats.probplot(x.values, dist="norm", plot=axes[1, 0])
    axes[1, 0].set_title("Normal Q-Q plot")

    plot_acf(x.values, lags=min(36, max(1, len(x) // 4)), ax=axes[1, 1], zero=False, color="#264653")
    axes[1, 1].set_title("Residual autocorrelation")

    fig.suptitle(f"SARIMAX residual diagnostics for {target_label}, train through {train_end.date()}", y=0.995)
    fig.tight_layout()
    stem = f"parametric_residual_diagnostics_{target}_train_through_{train_end.year}_12"
    for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
        fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"{stem}.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()

# ---- Nonparametric forecast execution: run scalar holdouts, save metrics, and save comparison plots ----

residual_rows = []
for target in ["statewide_rate", "max_h1_persistence"]:
    full_series = target_series_for_diagnostics(target)
    for train_end in [pd.Timestamp("2021-12-01"), pd.Timestamp("2022-12-01")]:
        train_series = full_series[full_series.index <= train_end]
        fit = fit_sarimax_for_target(train_series, target)
        residuals = pd.Series(np.asarray(fit.resid, dtype=float), index=train_series.index, name="residual")
        residual_rows.append(summarize_residual_normality(residuals, target, train_end))
        plot_residual_diagnostics(residuals, target, train_end)

parametric_residual_diagnostics = pd.DataFrame(residual_rows)
parametric_residual_diagnostics.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "parametric_sarimax_residual_normality_diagnostics.csv", index=False)
parametric_residual_diagnostics

# ---- Selected forecast figures: save the two visually useful residual-bootstrap forecast-band plots ----

NONPARAMETRIC_TARGETS = {
    "statewide_rate": {
        "label": "Statewide overdose deaths per capita",
        "value_column": "statewide_rate",
        "ylabel": "Deaths per capita",
    },
    "max_h1_persistence": {
        "label": "Maximum finite H1 persistence",
        "value_column": "max_h1_persistence",
        "ylabel": "Max finite H1 persistence",
    },
}


def target_series(target: str) -> pd.Series:
    """Load one scalar target series for nonparametric forecasting.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    if target == "statewide_rate":
        return statewide_series(completed_df).set_index("date")["statewide_rate"].asfreq("MS")
    if target == "max_h1_persistence":
        return observed_h1.set_index("date")["max_h1_persistence"].asfreq("MS")
    raise ValueError(target)


def empirical_interval_from_simulations(simulations: np.ndarray, point: np.ndarray) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Convert bootstrap simulations to point and quantile interval summaries.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    return pd.DataFrame({
        "predicted": point,
        "lower_50": np.quantile(simulations, 0.25, axis=0),
        "upper_50": np.quantile(simulations, 0.75, axis=0),
        "lower_80": np.quantile(simulations, 0.10, axis=0),
        "upper_80": np.quantile(simulations, 0.90, axis=0),
        "lower_90": np.quantile(simulations, 0.05, axis=0),
        "upper_90": np.quantile(simulations, 0.95, axis=0),
        "lower_95": np.quantile(simulations, 0.025, axis=0),
        "upper_95": np.quantile(simulations, 0.975, axis=0),
    })


def forecast_mean_bootstrap(train: pd.Series, horizon_dates: list[pd.Timestamp], rng: np.random.Generator, n_bootstrap: int = 3000) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Forecast from the training mean with residual-bootstrap intervals.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    y = train.dropna().astype(float)
    point = np.repeat(float(y.mean()), len(horizon_dates))
    residuals = (y - y.mean()).to_numpy(dtype=float)
    sims = point[None, :] + rng.choice(residuals, size=(n_bootstrap, len(horizon_dates)), replace=True)
    sims = np.maximum(sims, 0.0)
    out = empirical_interval_from_simulations(sims, point)
    out["model"] = "mean_residual_bootstrap"
    return out


def forecast_naive_bootstrap(train: pd.Series, horizon_dates: list[pd.Timestamp], rng: np.random.Generator, n_bootstrap: int = 3000) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Forecast by carrying the last value forward with bootstrapped innovations.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    y = train.dropna().astype(float)
    point = np.repeat(float(y.iloc[-1]), len(horizon_dates))
    one_step_residuals = y.diff().dropna().to_numpy(dtype=float)
    innovations = rng.choice(one_step_residuals, size=(n_bootstrap, len(horizon_dates)), replace=True)
    sims = point[None, :] + np.cumsum(innovations, axis=1)
    sims = np.maximum(sims, 0.0)
    out = empirical_interval_from_simulations(sims, point)
    out["model"] = "naive_residual_bootstrap"
    return out


def forecast_drift_bootstrap(train: pd.Series, horizon_dates: list[pd.Timestamp], rng: np.random.Generator, n_bootstrap: int = 3000) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Forecast with a drift term estimated from the training endpoints.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    y = train.dropna().astype(float)
    drift = float((y.iloc[-1] - y.iloc[0]) / max(len(y) - 1, 1))
    horizons = np.arange(1, len(horizon_dates) + 1)
    point = float(y.iloc[-1]) + horizons * drift
    one_step_residuals = (y.diff().dropna() - drift).to_numpy(dtype=float)
    innovations = rng.choice(one_step_residuals, size=(n_bootstrap, len(horizon_dates)), replace=True)
    sims = point[None, :] + np.cumsum(innovations, axis=1)
    sims = np.maximum(sims, 0.0)
    point = np.maximum(point, 0.0)
    out = empirical_interval_from_simulations(sims, point)
    out["model"] = "drift_residual_bootstrap"
    return out


def forecast_seasonal_naive_bootstrap(train: pd.Series, horizon_dates: list[pd.Timestamp], rng: np.random.Generator, n_bootstrap: int = 3000, period: int = 12) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Forecast each month from its most recent same-month observation.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    y = train.dropna().astype(float)
    point = []
    for date in horizon_dates:
        candidates = y[y.index.month == date.month]
        point.append(float(candidates.iloc[-1]))
    point = np.asarray(point, dtype=float)
    seasonal_residuals = (y - y.shift(period)).dropna().to_numpy(dtype=float)
    if len(seasonal_residuals) == 0:
        seasonal_residuals = (y - y.mean()).to_numpy(dtype=float)
    sims = point[None, :] + rng.choice(seasonal_residuals, size=(n_bootstrap, len(horizon_dates)), replace=True)
    sims = np.maximum(sims, 0.0)
    out = empirical_interval_from_simulations(sims, point)
    out["model"] = "seasonal_naive_residual_bootstrap"
    return out


def forecast_recent_same_month_bootstrap(train: pd.Series, horizon_dates: list[pd.Timestamp], rng: np.random.Generator, n_bootstrap: int = 3000, recent_years: int = 5) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Forecast from recent same-calendar-month averages.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    y = train.dropna().astype(float)
    point = []
    residuals = []
    for date in horizon_dates:
        candidates = y[y.index.month == date.month].tail(recent_years)
        month_mean = float(candidates.mean())
        point.append(month_mean)
        residuals.extend((candidates - month_mean).to_list())
    residuals = np.asarray(residuals, dtype=float)
    if len(residuals) < 4 or np.allclose(residuals, 0):
        residuals = (y - y.rolling(12, min_periods=1).mean()).dropna().to_numpy(dtype=float)
    point = np.asarray(point, dtype=float)
    sims = point[None, :] + rng.choice(residuals, size=(n_bootstrap, len(horizon_dates)), replace=True)
    sims = np.maximum(sims, 0.0)
    out = empirical_interval_from_simulations(sims, point)
    out["model"] = f"recent_{recent_years}_year_same_month_bootstrap"
    return out


def forecast_stl_loess_bootstrap(train: pd.Series, horizon_dates: list[pd.Timestamp], rng: np.random.Generator, n_bootstrap: int = 3000, period: int = 12) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Forecast from STL seasonality and a LOESS-smoothed local trend.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    y = train.dropna().astype(float).asfreq("MS")
    stl = STL(y, period=period, robust=True).fit()
    trend = pd.Series(stl.trend, index=y.index)
    seasonal = pd.Series(stl.seasonal, index=y.index)
    remainder = pd.Series(stl.resid, index=y.index).dropna().to_numpy(dtype=float)

    t = np.arange(len(y), dtype=float)
    smooth = lowess(y.to_numpy(dtype=float) - seasonal.to_numpy(dtype=float), t, frac=0.20, return_sorted=False)
    recent_diffs = np.diff(smooth[-min(36, len(smooth)):])
    local_drift = float(np.median(recent_diffs)) if len(recent_diffs) else 0.0
    horizons = np.arange(1, len(horizon_dates) + 1, dtype=float)
    trend_forecast = float(smooth[-1]) + horizons * local_drift

    month_map = seasonal.groupby(seasonal.index.month).apply(lambda s: float(s.tail(3).mean()))
    seasonal_forecast = np.asarray([month_map.loc[d.month] for d in horizon_dates], dtype=float)
    point = np.maximum(trend_forecast + seasonal_forecast, 0.0)
    if len(remainder) < 4 or np.allclose(remainder, 0):
        remainder = (y - y.mean()).to_numpy(dtype=float)
    sims = point[None, :] + rng.choice(remainder, size=(n_bootstrap, len(horizon_dates)), replace=True)
    sims = np.maximum(sims, 0.0)
    out = empirical_interval_from_simulations(sims, point)
    out["model"] = "stl_loess_residual_bootstrap"
    return out


def run_nonparametric_holdout(target: str, train_end: pd.Timestamp, holdout_year: int, n_bootstrap: int = 3000) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
    """Run nonparametric scalar forecasts for one target and holdout year.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    y = target_series(target)
    train = y[y.index <= train_end]
    observed = y[(y.index >= pd.Timestamp(f"{holdout_year}-01-01")) & (y.index <= pd.Timestamp(f"{holdout_year}-12-01"))]
    horizon_dates = list(observed.index)
    seed_offset = 10_000 + holdout_year + (0 if target == "statewide_rate" else 100)
    rng = np.random.default_rng(CONFIG["random_seed"] + seed_offset)
    frames = []
    for fn in [
        forecast_mean_bootstrap,
        forecast_naive_bootstrap,
        forecast_drift_bootstrap,
        forecast_seasonal_naive_bootstrap,
        forecast_recent_same_month_bootstrap,
        forecast_stl_loess_bootstrap,
    ]:
        frame = fn(train, horizon_dates, rng, n_bootstrap=n_bootstrap)
        frame["target"] = target
        frame["train_end"] = train_end
        frame["holdout_year"] = holdout_year
        frame["horizon"] = np.arange(1, len(horizon_dates) + 1)
        frame["date"] = horizon_dates
        frame["observed"] = observed.to_numpy(dtype=float)
        frames.append(frame)
    forecasts = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    metrics = []
    for model_name, group in forecasts.groupby("model"):
        metrics.append({
            "target": target,
            "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
            "holdout_year": holdout_year,
            "model": model_name,
            "mae": mae(group["observed"], group["predicted"]),
            "rmse": rmse(group["observed"], group["predicted"]),
            "coverage_80": float(np.mean((group["observed"] >= group["lower_80"]) & (group["observed"] <= group["upper_80"]))),
            "coverage_95": float(np.mean((group["observed"] >= group["lower_95"]) & (group["observed"] <= group["upper_95"]))),
        })
    return forecasts, pd.DataFrame(metrics)


def plot_nonparametric_best_forecast(forecasts: pd.DataFrame, metrics: pd.DataFrame, target: str, holdout_year: int) -> None:
    """Save the lowest-RMSE nonparametric forecast plot.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    best = metrics.sort_values(["rmse", "mae"], ascending=[True, True]).iloc[0]
    model_name = best["model"]
    plot_df = forecasts[forecasts["model"].eq(model_name)].sort_values("date")
    y = target_series(target)
    train_end = pd.Timestamp(best["train_end"])
    history = y[(y.index >= train_end - pd.DateOffset(years=5)) & (y.index <= train_end)]
    observed_holdout = pd.Series(plot_df["observed"].to_numpy(dtype=float), index=pd.to_datetime(plot_df["date"]))
    observed_line = pd.concat([history.tail(1), observed_holdout])
    label = NONPARAMETRIC_TARGETS[target]["label"]
    ylabel = NONPARAMETRIC_TARGETS[target]["ylabel"]
    readable_model = model_name.replace("_", " ")

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.8))
    ax.plot(history.index, history.values, color="#111111", lw=1.4, alpha=0.75, label="Training history")
    ax.plot(observed_line.index, observed_line.values, color="#111111", marker="o", lw=1.8, label="Observed through holdout")
    ax.plot(plot_df["date"], plot_df["predicted"], color="#2a9d8f", marker="o", lw=1.7, label=f"Forecast: {readable_model}")
    ax.fill_between(plot_df["date"], plot_df["lower_80"], plot_df["upper_80"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.18, label="80% bootstrap interval")
    ax.fill_between(plot_df["date"], plot_df["lower_95"], plot_df["upper_95"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.09, label="95% bootstrap interval")
    ax.axvline(train_end, color="#666666", lw=1, ls=":")
    ax.set_title(f"Lowest-RMSE nonparametric forecast for {label}, {holdout_year} holdout\nSelected model: {readable_model}")
    ax.set_xlabel("Month")
    ax.set_ylabel(ylabel)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    fig.tight_layout()
    stem = f"nonparametric_{target}_holdout_{holdout_year}_forecast_band"
    for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
        fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"{stem}.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()


def plot_nonparametric_model_comparison(forecasts: pd.DataFrame, target: str, holdout_year: int) -> None:
    """Save a point-forecast comparison plot across nonparametric models.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    label = NONPARAMETRIC_TARGETS[target]["label"]
    ylabel = NONPARAMETRIC_TARGETS[target]["ylabel"]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.8))
    observed = forecasts.drop_duplicates("date").sort_values("date")
    train_end = pd.Timestamp(observed["train_end"].iloc[0])
    y = target_series(target)
    history = y[(y.index >= train_end - pd.DateOffset(years=5)) & (y.index <= train_end)]
    observed_holdout = pd.Series(observed["observed"].to_numpy(dtype=float), index=pd.to_datetime(observed["date"]))
    observed_line = pd.concat([history.tail(1), observed_holdout])
    ax.plot(history.index, history.values, color="#111111", lw=1.1, alpha=0.55, label="Training history")
    ax.plot(observed_line.index, observed_line.values, color="#111111", marker="o", lw=1.8, label="Observed through holdout")
    colors = ["#457b9d", "#2a9d8f", "#e76f51", "#a23b72", "#6a4c93", "#8d99ae"]
    for color, (model_name, group) in zip(colors, forecasts.groupby("model")):
        group = group.sort_values("date")
        ax.plot(group["date"], group["predicted"], lw=1.2, alpha=0.9, color=color, label=model_name.replace("_", " "))
    ax.axvline(train_end, color="#666666", lw=1, ls=":")
    ax.set_title(f"Nonparametric point forecast comparison for {label}, {holdout_year} holdout")
    ax.set_xlabel("Held-out month")
    ax.set_ylabel(ylabel)
    ax.legend(fontsize=7, ncol=2)
    fig.tight_layout()
    stem = f"nonparametric_{target}_holdout_{holdout_year}_model_comparison"
    for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
        fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"{stem}.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()
```


The function below loads the two scalar time series that we will forecast: statewide deaths per capita and maximum $H_1$ persistence.

```{python}
#| label: scalar-series-loaders
def parse_month_label(label: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
    """Parse a wide monthly column label of the form year_month.

    Args:
        label: Month label such as ``2007_1``.

    Returns:
        A pair ``(year, month)``.
    """
    year_text, month_text = str(label).split("_")
    return int(year_text), int(month_text)


def canonical_county_name(series: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    """Normalize county labels so the data files and Ohio complex agree.

    Args:
        series: County-name column.

    Returns:
        County names with suffixes and spacing normalized.
    """
    return (
        series.astype(str)
        .str.replace(r"\s+County,\s*OH$", "", regex=True)
        .str.replace(r"\s+County$", "", regex=True)
        .str.strip()
        .replace({"Van Wert": "VanWert"})
    )


def load_statewide_rate_series() -> pd.Series:
    """Construct the statewide deaths-per-capita series.

    Returns:
        Monthly statewide overdose deaths divided by statewide population.
    """
    counts = pd.read_csv(FORECAST_DIR.parent / "data" / "county_drug_death.csv")
    if "county" not in counts.columns:
        counts = counts.rename(columns={counts.columns[0]: "county"})
    counts["county"] = canonical_county_name(counts["county"])
    counts = counts[~counts["county"].str.lower().isin({"total", "ohio", "statewide"})].copy()

    pop_path = FORECAST_DIR.parent / "data" / "ohio_county_populations.csv"
    pop = pd.read_csv(pop_path)
    if "county" not in pop.columns:
        pop = pop.rename(columns={pop.columns[0]: "county"})
    pop["county"] = canonical_county_name(pop["county"])

    rows = []
    month_cols = [c for c in counts.columns if "_" in str(c)]
    for col in month_cols:
        year, month = parse_month_label(col)
        deaths = pd.to_numeric(counts[col], errors="coerce").sum()
        if str(year) in pop.columns:
            population = pd.to_numeric(pop[str(year)], errors="coerce").sum()
        else:
            population = np.nan
        rows.append({"date": pd.Timestamp(year=year, month=month, day=1), "statewide_rate": deaths / population})
    series = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("date").set_index("date")["statewide_rate"]
    return series.asfreq("MS")


def load_max_h1_series() -> pd.Series:
    """Load the historical maximum finite H1 persistence series.

    Returns:
        Monthly maximum finite H1 persistence indexed by month.
    """
    h1 = read_derived_csv("historical_h1_persistence_summary.csv", parse_dates=["date"])
    value_col = "max_h1_persistence" if "max_h1_persistence" in h1.columns else "observed_max_h1_persistence"
    return h1.sort_values("date").set_index("date")[value_col].astype(float).asfreq("MS")

statewide_rate_series = load_statewide_rate_series()
max_h1_series = load_max_h1_series()

```

## Checking our SARIMA Models

To keep this file focused on forecasting, we did our model fitting elsewhere. We made ACFs, PACFs, used the auto.arima() function, and tried several models. Below, we present certain good models, show that their residuals appear independent, and compare them using AIC.

The next cell fits a small SARIMA candidate set for each scalar target and each main cutoff. It reports AIC and the Ljung-Box p-value at lag 24. We keep a candidate only if the numerical fit converges and the Ljung-Box p-value is at least 0.05. This is not a proof that the residuals are independent, but it is a useful screen against obvious leftover autocorrelation. The scalar series are rescaled before fitting to avoid numerical problems caused by very small deaths-per-capita and persistence values.

```{python}
#| label: sarima-candidate-selection
SARIMA_CANDIDATES = [
    {"name": "SARIMA(1,0,0)(1,0,0)[12]", "order": (1, 0, 0), "seasonal_order": (1, 0, 0, 12), "trend": "c"},
    {"name": "SARIMA(1,0,1)(1,0,0)[12]", "order": (1, 0, 1), "seasonal_order": (1, 0, 0, 12), "trend": "c"},
    {"name": "SARIMA(1,1,1)(1,0,1)[12]", "order": (1, 1, 1), "seasonal_order": (1, 0, 1, 12), "trend": "c"},
    {"name": "SARIMA(0,1,1)(0,1,1)[12]", "order": (0, 1, 1), "seasonal_order": (0, 1, 1, 12), "trend": "n"},
    {"name": "SARIMA(0,1,1)(1,0,0)[12]", "order": (0, 1, 1), "seasonal_order": (1, 0, 0, 12), "trend": "n"},
]


def fit_sarima_candidates(series: pd.Series, target: str, train_end: pd.Timestamp) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Fit SARIMA candidates and summarize residual diagnostics.

    Args:
        series: Scalar monthly time series to model.
        target: Name of the scalar target.
        train_end: Last month included in the training period.

    Returns:
        One row per SARIMA candidate with AIC, convergence status, and Ljung-Box results.
    """
    raw = series[series.index <= train_end].dropna().astype(float)
    scale = 100_000.0
    y = raw * scale
    rows = []
    for spec in SARIMA_CANDIDATES:
        try:
            model = SARIMAX(
                y,
                order=spec["order"],
                seasonal_order=spec["seasonal_order"],
                trend=spec["trend"],
                enforce_stationarity=False,
                enforce_invertibility=False,
            )
            result = model.fit(disp=False, maxiter=500)
            resid = pd.Series(result.resid, index=y.index).replace([np.inf, -np.inf], np.nan).dropna()
            lag = min(24, max(1, len(resid) // 5))
            lb = acorr_ljungbox(resid, lags=[lag], return_df=True)
            converged = bool(getattr(result, "mle_retvals", {}).get("converged", True))
            lb_pass = bool(lb["lb_pvalue"].iloc[0] >= 0.05)
            rows.append({
                "target": target,
                "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
                "model": spec["name"],
                "scale_used_for_fit": scale,
                "aic": float(result.aic),
                "bic": float(result.bic),
                "ljung_box_lag": lag,
                "ljung_box_pvalue": float(lb["lb_pvalue"].iloc[0]),
                "converged": converged,
                "passes_ljung_box_0_05": lb_pass,
                "passes_sarima_screen": bool(converged and lb_pass),
            })
        except Exception as exc:
            rows.append({
                "target": target,
                "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
                "model": spec["name"],
                "scale_used_for_fit": scale,
                "aic": np.nan,
                "bic": np.nan,
                "ljung_box_lag": np.nan,
                "ljung_box_pvalue": np.nan,
                "converged": False,
                "passes_ljung_box_0_05": False,
                "passes_sarima_screen": False,
                "error": str(exc),
            })
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)

sarima_selection = pd.concat([
    fit_sarima_candidates(statewide_rate_series, "statewide_rate", pd.Timestamp("2021-12-01")),
    fit_sarima_candidates(statewide_rate_series, "statewide_rate", pd.Timestamp("2022-12-01")),
    fit_sarima_candidates(max_h1_series, "max_h1_persistence", pd.Timestamp("2021-12-01")),
    fit_sarima_candidates(max_h1_series, "max_h1_persistence", pd.Timestamp("2022-12-01")),
], ignore_index=True)

sarima_selection.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "sarima_candidate_selection.csv", index=False)
retained_sarima_selection = sarima_selection[
    sarima_selection["passes_sarima_screen"].fillna(False)
].copy()
retained_sarima_selection = retained_sarima_selection[[
    "target", "train_end", "model", "aic", "ljung_box_lag", "ljung_box_pvalue",
    "converged", "passes_ljung_box_0_05", "passes_sarima_screen"
]].sort_values(["target", "train_end", "aic"])
retained_sarima_selection
```

The table above shows several strong candidates: models that converged and passed the Ljung-Box test. We compare the models using AIC and forecast accuracy below. For selected models, we illustrate how to check the residuals for normality.

```{python}
#| label: sarimax-residual-diagnostic-data
parametric_residual_diagnostics = read_derived_csv("parametric_sarimax_residual_normality_diagnostics.csv")
```

```{python}
#| label: sarimax-residual-diagnostic-summary
parametric_residual_diagnostics
```

The selected residual plots below come from the source code in this section. Broadly speaking, they show that the residuals do look random, the ACF plot shows no further temporal autocorrelation, and the residuals appear normal. Hence, when forecasting from these models, the normal distribution can be used to create forecasting intervals (although when we forecast below, we use a non-parametric bootstrapping approach that also works for our non-parametric forecasts).

```{python}
#| label: sarimax-residual-diagnostic-figures
for name in [
    "parametric_residual_diagnostics_statewide_rate_train_through_2021_12.png",
    "parametric_residual_diagnostics_statewide_rate_train_through_2022_12.png",
    "parametric_residual_diagnostics_max_h1_persistence_train_through_2021_12.png",
    "parametric_residual_diagnostics_max_h1_persistence_train_through_2022_12.png",
]:
    display(Image(filename=str(FIGURE_DIR / name)))
```

## Residual-Bootstrap Forecast Intervals

The selected forecast figures below use residual-bootstrap prediction intervals, which we now explain. The starting identity is

$$
y_t = \hat y_t + e_t.
$$

For a future month, the forecasting rule gives $\hat y_{T+h}$, but the future error $e_{T+h}$ is unknown. The bootstrap idea is to use the fitted residuals from the training period as an empirical stand-in for future forecast errors. We repeatedly sample residuals with replacement, add them to the point forecast, and obtain many possible future values for each horizon. The interval is then read from empirical quantiles of those simulated future values. A 90% interval, for example, runs from the 5th to the 95th percentiles, cutting off the most extreme 5% from each tail.

These prediction intervals do not require residual normality. They do require the training residuals to be a reasonable picture of future forecast errors. If residuals have strong remaining autocorrelation, changing variance, or a few dominant outliers, then a simple residual bootstrap can be misleading. This is why we still inspect residual plots even when we do not use normal-theory intervals.

## Cross Validation Metrics for Forecast Accuracy

We use completed-year cross validation. Experiment A trains through December 2021 and forecasts January-December 2022. This is most defensible, since the 2023 data was still preliminary when we downloaded it. Experiment B trains through December 2022 and forecasts January-December 2023. This is valuable, but it's hard to check if the forecasts are good, since the 2023 data was preliminary and might have changed due to death investigations. For this reason, when looking at the overdose data, we report Experiment A.

The main tracking number is `heldout_correlation_squared`, the squared Pearson correlation between the 12 held-out observations and the 12 point forecasts. This number is between 0 and 1 when the correlation is defined. It asks whether the forecast follows the month-to-month pattern in the held-out year. We also report MAE and RMSE, which measure the size of the forecast errors in the original units, and coverage columns for intervals. The columns `coverage_80` and `coverage_95` are the fractions of holdout months whose observed value lies inside the 80% and 95% prediction intervals. Since the 80% interval is narrower, its coverage can be smaller than the 95% coverage.

The next code block runs the holdout experiment, i.e., tests the models above (trained on the training data) on the holdout test data.

```{python}
#| label: cross-validation-output-source
#| eval: false
def run_holdout_experiment(spec: dict[str, Any], base_seed: int) -> dict[str, pd.DataFrame | CountModelFit]:
    """Run one completed-year training/holdout forecasting experiment.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        A dictionary of prediction, metric, diagnostic, and model-summary dataframes.
    """
    experiment = spec["experiment"]
    train_end = spec["train_end"]
    holdout_start = spec["holdout_start"]
    holdout_end = spec["holdout_end"]
    train = df_all[df_all["date"] <= train_end].copy()
    holdout = df_all[(df_all["date"] >= holdout_start) & (df_all["date"] <= holdout_end)].copy()
    holdout_dates = sorted(holdout["date"].unique())
    hotspot_threshold = choose_hotspot_threshold(train)
    rng = np.random.default_rng(base_seed)

    train_state = statewide_series(train)
    observed_state = statewide_series(holdout)[["date", "statewide_rate", "statewide_deaths"]]
    state_preds = pd.concat([
        baseline_statewide_forecasts(train_state, holdout_dates),
        sarima_statewide_forecast(train_state, holdout_dates),
    ], ignore_index=True)

    fit = fit_count_model(train)
    sims = simulate_paths(train, fit, len(holdout_dates), CONFIG["n_holdout_simulations"], rng, observed_df_for_population=holdout)
    county_pred, expected_counts = summarize_county_simulations(sims, hotspot_threshold)
    model_state = summarize_statewide_from_county_sims(sims)
    model_state = model_state.rename(columns={"statewide_rate_lower_80": "lower_80", "statewide_rate_upper_80": "upper_80", "statewide_rate_lower_95": "lower_95", "statewide_rate_upper_95": "upper_95"})
    model_state["model"] = "county_negative_binomial_induced_statewide"
    model_state = model_state[["model", "horizon", "date", "predicted_rate", "predicted_median_rate", "lower_80", "upper_80", "lower_95", "upper_95"]]
    state_preds = pd.concat([state_preds, model_state], ignore_index=True)
    state_preds = state_preds.merge(observed_state, on="date", how="left", validate="many_to_one")
    state_preds["experiment"] = experiment
    state_preds["train_end"] = train_end
    state_preds["holdout_start"] = holdout_start
    state_preds["holdout_end"] = holdout_end

    state_metrics = []
    for model_name, group in state_preds.dropna(subset=["predicted_rate"]).groupby("model"):
        observed_sd = float(group["statewide_rate"].std(ddof=0))
        predicted_sd = float(group["predicted_rate"].std(ddof=0))
        correlation_defined = len(group) > 1 and observed_sd > 1e-15 and predicted_sd > 1e-15
        corr = group[["statewide_rate", "predicted_rate"]].corr().iloc[0, 1] if correlation_defined else np.nan
        state_metrics.append({
            "experiment": experiment,
            "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
            "holdout_year": int(holdout_start.year),
            "model": model_name,
            "mae_statewide_rate": mae(group["statewide_rate"], group["predicted_rate"]),
            "rmse_statewide_rate": rmse(group["statewide_rate"], group["predicted_rate"]),
            "observed_sd_statewide_rate": observed_sd,
            "predicted_sd_statewide_rate": predicted_sd,
            "correlation_squared_defined": bool(correlation_defined),
            "correlation_squared_statewide_rate": float(corr ** 2) if np.isfinite(corr) else np.nan,
        })

    obs_county = holdout[["county", "date", "deaths", "deaths_per_capita"]].rename(columns={"deaths": "observed_deaths", "deaths_per_capita": "observed_rate"})
    county_joined = county_pred.merge(obs_county, on=["county", "date"], how="left", validate="one_to_one")
    if county_joined[["observed_deaths", "observed_rate"]].isna().any().any():
        raise ValueError(f"Holdout {experiment} has incomplete county observed joins.")
    county_joined["experiment"] = experiment
    county_joined["train_end"] = train_end
    county_joined["holdout_year"] = int(holdout_start.year)

    county_metrics = pd.DataFrame([{
        "experiment": experiment,
        "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
        "holdout_year": int(holdout_start.year),
        "model": "county_negative_binomial",
        "n_county_months": len(county_joined),
        "n_simulations": CONFIG["n_holdout_simulations"],
        "hotspot_threshold": hotspot_threshold,
        "mae_deaths": mae(county_joined["observed_deaths"], county_joined["expected_deaths"]),
        "rmse_deaths": rmse(county_joined["observed_deaths"], county_joined["expected_deaths"]),
        "mae_rate": mae(county_joined["observed_rate"], county_joined["expected_rate"]),
        "rmse_rate": rmse(county_joined["observed_rate"], county_joined["expected_rate"]),
        "rate_correlation": float(county_joined[["expected_rate", "observed_rate"]].corr().iloc[0, 1]),
        "deaths_coverage_50": float(np.mean((county_joined["observed_deaths"] >= county_joined["deaths_lower_50"]) & (county_joined["observed_deaths"] <= county_joined["deaths_upper_50"]))),
        "deaths_coverage_80": float(np.mean((county_joined["observed_deaths"] >= county_joined["deaths_lower_80"]) & (county_joined["observed_deaths"] <= county_joined["deaths_upper_80"]))),
        "deaths_coverage_95": float(np.mean((county_joined["observed_deaths"] >= county_joined["deaths_lower_95"]) & (county_joined["observed_deaths"] <= county_joined["deaths_upper_95"]))),
        "rate_coverage_50": float(np.mean((county_joined["observed_rate"] >= county_joined["rate_lower_50"]) & (county_joined["observed_rate"] <= county_joined["rate_upper_50"]))),
        "rate_coverage_80": float(np.mean((county_joined["observed_rate"] >= county_joined["rate_lower_80"]) & (county_joined["observed_rate"] <= county_joined["rate_upper_80"]))),
        "rate_coverage_95": float(np.mean((county_joined["observed_rate"] >= county_joined["rate_lower_95"]) & (county_joined["observed_rate"] <= county_joined["rate_upper_95"]))),
    }])

    observed_exceeds = county_joined["observed_rate"] > hotspot_threshold
    county_joined["observed_exceeds_hotspot_threshold"] = observed_exceeds
    brier = float(np.mean((county_joined["exceedance_probability"] - observed_exceeds.astype(float)) ** 2))
    exceedance_metrics = []
    sim_counts = sims.assign(exceeds_hotspot_threshold=lambda x: x["deaths_per_capita"] > hotspot_threshold).groupby("simulation").agg(n_exceeding_county_months=("exceeds_hotspot_threshold", "sum")).reset_index()
    for cutoff in [0.25, 0.50, 0.75]:
        predicted = county_joined["exceedance_probability"] >= cutoff
        tp = int((predicted & observed_exceeds).sum())
        fp = int((predicted & ~observed_exceeds).sum())
        fn = int((~predicted & observed_exceeds).sum())
        exceedance_metrics.append({
            "experiment": experiment,
            "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
            "holdout_year": int(holdout_start.year),
            "model": "county_negative_binomial",
            "hotspot_threshold": hotspot_threshold,
            "probability_cutoff": cutoff,
            "brier_score": brier,
            "observed_exceeding_county_months": int(observed_exceeds.sum()),
            "predicted_mean_exceeding_county_months": float(sim_counts["n_exceeding_county_months"].mean()),
            "predicted_median_exceeding_county_months": float(sim_counts["n_exceeding_county_months"].median()),
            "predicted_lower_80_exceeding_county_months": float(sim_counts["n_exceeding_county_months"].quantile(0.10)),
            "predicted_upper_80_exceeding_county_months": float(sim_counts["n_exceeding_county_months"].quantile(0.90)),
            "precision": float(tp / max(tp + fp, 1)),
            "recall": float(tp / max(tp + fn, 1)),
            "n_predicted_positive": int(predicted.sum()),
        })

    bins = pd.IntervalIndex.from_tuples([(0.0, 0.10), (0.10, 0.25), (0.25, 0.50), (0.50, 0.75), (0.75, 1.0)], closed="right")
    calibration = county_joined.copy()
    calibration["probability_bin"] = pd.cut(calibration["exceedance_probability"], bins=bins, include_lowest=True)
    calibration = calibration.groupby("probability_bin", observed=True).agg(
        n=("county", "size"),
        mean_predicted_probability=("exceedance_probability", "mean"),
        observed_frequency=("observed_exceeds_hotspot_threshold", "mean"),
    ).reset_index()
    calibration["experiment"] = experiment
    calibration["holdout_year"] = int(holdout_start.year)
    calibration["bin_left"] = calibration["probability_bin"].map(lambda x: x.left)
    calibration["bin_right"] = calibration["probability_bin"].map(lambda x: x.right)
    calibration = calibration.drop(columns=["probability_bin"])

    sim_h1, sim_ranked_h1, h1_failures = compute_simulated_h1(sims, ps, county_order, CONFIG["top_rank_count"], f"holdout_{experiment}_{holdout_start.year}")
    h1_summary, ranked_summary = summarize_h1_simulations(sim_h1, sim_ranked_h1, h1_threshold)
    obs_h1 = observed_h1[observed_h1["date"].isin(holdout_dates)][["date", "max_h1_persistence", "h1_point_count", "top_ranked_total_persistence"]].rename(columns={"max_h1_persistence": "observed_max_h1_persistence"})
    h1_joined = h1_summary.merge(obs_h1, on="date", how="left", validate="one_to_one")
    h1_joined["experiment"] = experiment
    h1_joined["holdout_year"] = int(holdout_start.year)
    for level in ["50", "80", "95"]:
        h1_joined[f"max_h1_coverage_{level}"] = (h1_joined["observed_max_h1_persistence"] >= h1_joined[f"max_h1_lower_{level}"]) & (h1_joined["observed_max_h1_persistence"] <= h1_joined[f"max_h1_upper_{level}"])

    h1_metrics = pd.DataFrame([{
        "experiment": experiment,
        "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
        "holdout_year": int(holdout_start.year),
        "model": "county_negative_binomial_induced_h1",
        "mae_max_h1": mae(h1_joined["observed_max_h1_persistence"], h1_joined["max_h1_median"]),
        "rmse_max_h1": rmse(h1_joined["observed_max_h1_persistence"], h1_joined["max_h1_median"]),
        "coverage_50_max_h1": float(h1_joined["max_h1_coverage_50"].mean()),
        "coverage_80_max_h1": float(h1_joined["max_h1_coverage_80"].mean()),
        "coverage_95_max_h1": float(h1_joined["max_h1_coverage_95"].mean()),
        "h1_failure_count": len(h1_failures),
        "h1_failure_rate": len(h1_failures) / max(CONFIG["n_holdout_simulations"] * len(holdout_dates), 1),
    }])

    direct = direct_h1_scalar_forecast(observed_h1[observed_h1["date"] <= train_end].copy(), holdout_dates, h1_threshold)
    direct = direct.merge(obs_h1[["date", "observed_max_h1_persistence"]], on="date", how="left")
    direct["experiment"] = experiment
    direct_metrics = []
    for model_name, group in direct.dropna(subset=["forecast_mean"]).groupby("model"):
        direct_metrics.append({
            "experiment": experiment,
            "train_end": train_end.date().isoformat(),
            "holdout_year": int(holdout_start.year),
            "model": model_name,
            "mae_max_h1": mae(group["observed_max_h1_persistence"], group["forecast_mean"]),
            "rmse_max_h1": rmse(group["observed_max_h1_persistence"], group["forecast_mean"]),
            "coverage_80_max_h1": float(np.mean((group["observed_max_h1_persistence"] >= group["lower_80"]) & (group["observed_max_h1_persistence"] <= group["upper_80"]))),
            "coverage_95_max_h1": float(np.mean((group["observed_max_h1_persistence"] >= group["lower_95"]) & (group["observed_max_h1_persistence"] <= group["upper_95"]))),
        })
    h1_metrics = pd.concat([h1_metrics, pd.DataFrame(direct_metrics)], ignore_index=True)

    expected_counts["experiment"] = experiment
    expected_counts["holdout_year"] = int(holdout_start.year)
    ranked_summary["experiment"] = experiment
    ranked_summary["holdout_year"] = int(holdout_start.year)
    direct["holdout_year"] = int(holdout_start.year)

    model_summary = pd.DataFrame([{
        "experiment": experiment,
        "fit_label": f"train_through_{train_end.date().isoformat()}",
        "response": "county-month overdose death count",
        "distribution": "negative binomial",
        "exposure_offset": "log county population",
        "county_effects": "county fixed effects",
        "seasonality": "month-of-year fixed effects",
        "time_trend": "linear monthly time index",
        "lag_terms": "log(1 + county lag deaths), lagged county deaths per capita, log(1 + statewide lag deaths)",
        "method": fit.method,
        "formula": fit.formula,
        "alpha": fit.alpha,
        "training_rows": fit.n_train_rows,
        "training_months": fit.n_train_months,
        "train_start": fit.train_start.date().isoformat(),
        "train_end": fit.train_end.date().isoformat(),
        "forecast_horizon_months": len(holdout_dates),
        "simulations": CONFIG["n_holdout_simulations"],
        "hotspot_threshold": hotspot_threshold,
        "converged": fit.converged,
        "aic": float(getattr(fit.result, "aic", np.nan)),
    }])

    return {
        "statewide_predictions": state_preds,
        "statewide_metrics": pd.DataFrame(state_metrics),
        "county_predictions": county_joined,
        "county_metrics": county_metrics,
        "exceedance_metrics": pd.DataFrame(exceedance_metrics),
        "exceedance_calibration": calibration,
        "expected_counts": expected_counts,
        "sim_h1": sim_h1,
        "h1_ranked_summary": ranked_summary,
        "h1_metrics": h1_metrics,
        "h1_predictions": h1_joined,
        "direct_h1_predictions": direct,
        "model_summary": model_summary,
        "h1_failures": h1_failures,
        "fit": fit,
    }

# ---- Cross-validation runner: fit models on completed training windows and score the held-out year ----

holdout_outputs = []
for i, spec in enumerate(CONFIG["holdouts"]):
    print(f"Running holdout experiment {spec['experiment']}: train through {spec['train_end'].date()}, forecast {spec['holdout_start'].year}")
    holdout_outputs.append(run_holdout_experiment(spec, CONFIG["random_seed"] + 1000 * (i + 1)))

statewide_metrics = pd.concat([x["statewide_metrics"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
statewide_predictions = pd.concat([x["statewide_predictions"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
county_metrics = pd.concat([x["county_metrics"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
county_predictions = pd.concat([x["county_predictions"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
exceedance_metrics = pd.concat([x["exceedance_metrics"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
exceedance_calibration = pd.concat([x["exceedance_calibration"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
expected_counts_holdout = pd.concat([x["expected_counts"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
h1_metrics = pd.concat([x["h1_metrics"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
h1_predictions = pd.concat([x["h1_predictions"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
h1_ranked_holdout = pd.concat([x["h1_ranked_summary"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
direct_h1_predictions = pd.concat([x["direct_h1_predictions"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
model_summaries = pd.concat([x["model_summary"] for x in holdout_outputs], ignore_index=True)
h1_failures_holdout = pd.concat([x["h1_failures"] for x in holdout_outputs if len(x["h1_failures"])], ignore_index=True) if any(len(x["h1_failures"]) for x in holdout_outputs) else pd.DataFrame()

statewide_metrics.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "statewide_forecast_cv_metrics.csv", index=False)
statewide_predictions[statewide_predictions["holdout_year" if "holdout_year" in statewide_predictions.columns else "experiment"].notna() if False else statewide_predictions.index >= 0].to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "statewide_forecast_holdouts_all.csv", index=False)
county_metrics.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "county_forecast_cv_metrics.csv", index=False)
county_predictions.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "county_forecast_holdouts_all.csv", index=False)
exceedance_metrics.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "exceedance_forecast_cv_metrics.csv", index=False)
exceedance_calibration.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "exceedance_forecast_calibration.csv", index=False)
expected_counts_holdout.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "expected_exceeding_counties_holdouts_all.csv", index=False)
h1_metrics.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "h1_forecast_cv_metrics.csv", index=False)
h1_predictions.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "h1_model_induced_holdouts_all.csv", index=False)
h1_ranked_holdout.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "h1_ranked_holdout_summary_all.csv", index=False)
direct_h1_predictions.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "direct_h1_holdout_predictions.csv", index=False)
model_summaries.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "forecast_model_summary.csv", index=False)
h1_failures_holdout.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "h1_holdout_failures.csv", index=False)

for year in [2022, 2023]:
    statewide_predictions[statewide_predictions["date"].dt.year == year].to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / f"statewide_forecast_holdout_{year}.csv", index=False)
    county_predictions[county_predictions["date"].dt.year == year].to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / f"county_forecast_holdout_{year}.csv", index=False)
    county_predictions[county_predictions["date"].dt.year == year][["experiment", "holdout_year", "date", "county", "hotspot_threshold", "exceedance_probability", "observed_exceeds_hotspot_threshold", "observed_rate", "expected_rate"]].to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / f"county_exceedance_holdout_{year}.csv", index=False)
    h1_predictions[h1_predictions["date"].dt.year == year].to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / f"h1_model_induced_holdout_{year}.csv", index=False)

statewide_metrics.sort_values(["holdout_year", "rmse_statewide_rate"])

# ---- Holdout execution and saved CSV outputs for completed-year cross validation ----

statewide_display_columns = [
    "experiment",
    "train_end",
    "holdout_year",
    "model",
    "mae_statewide_rate",
    "rmse_statewide_rate",
    "predicted_sd_statewide_rate",
    "correlation_squared_defined",
]
h1_display_columns = [
    "experiment",
    "train_end",
    "holdout_year",
    "model",
    "mae_max_h1",
    "rmse_max_h1",
    "coverage_80_max_h1",
    "coverage_95_max_h1",
]
print("Statewide forecast metrics")
display(statewide_metrics.sort_values(["holdout_year", "rmse_statewide_rate"])[statewide_display_columns])
print("County negative-binomial metrics")
display(county_metrics)
print("Hotspot exceedance metrics")
display(exceedance_metrics.sort_values(["holdout_year", "probability_cutoff"]))
print("H1 forecast metrics")
display(h1_metrics.sort_values(["holdout_year", "model", "rmse_max_h1"])[h1_display_columns])

# ---- Cross-validation figures: statewide rates, county rates, exceedance calibration, expected exceeding counties, and induced max H1 ----

for year in [2022, 2023]:
    plot_df = statewide_predictions[statewide_predictions["date"].dt.year == year].copy()
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.8))
    observed = plot_df.drop_duplicates("date").sort_values("date")
    ax.plot(observed["date"], observed["statewide_rate"], color="#111111", marker="o", lw=1.8, label="Observed")
    for model_name, color in [("seasonal_naive", "#457b9d"), ("sarimax_statewide_rate", "#e76f51"), ("county_negative_binomial_induced_statewide", "#2a9d8f")]:
        sub = plot_df[plot_df["model"].eq(model_name)].sort_values("date")
        if sub.empty:
            continue
        label = model_name.replace("_", " ")
        ax.plot(sub["date"], sub["predicted_rate"], marker=".", lw=1.4, label=label, color=color)
        if model_name == "county_negative_binomial_induced_statewide" and sub[["lower_80", "upper_80"]].notna().all().all():
            ax.fill_between(sub["date"], sub["lower_80"], sub["upper_80"], color=color, alpha=0.15, label="county model 80% interval")
            ax.fill_between(sub["date"], sub["lower_95"], sub["upper_95"], color=color, alpha=0.08, label="county model 95% interval")
    ax.set_title(f"Statewide rate forecast for {year} holdout")
    ax.set_xlabel("Held-out month")
    ax.set_ylabel("Deaths per capita")
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    fig.tight_layout()
    for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
        fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"statewide_rate_holdout_{year}_forecast_band.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: cross-validation-metric-data
statewide_metrics = read_derived_csv("statewide_forecast_cv_metrics.csv")
h1_metrics = read_derived_csv("h1_forecast_cv_metrics.csv")
nonparametric_metrics = read_derived_csv("nonparametric_scalar_forecast_cv_metrics.csv")
nonparametric_holdouts = read_derived_csv("nonparametric_scalar_forecast_holdouts.csv", parse_dates=["date", "train_end"])
direct_h1_holdouts = read_derived_csv("direct_h1_holdout_predictions.csv", parse_dates=["date"])
```

The table below keeps the statewide models that are easiest to interpret. MAE and RMSE measure forecast error in deaths per capita. The column `heldout_correlation_squared` is the squared Pearson correlation between the 12 held-out observations and the 12 point forecasts. It is the main tracking number in this section: it asks whether a forecast follows the relative month-to-month pattern in the held-out year.

```{python}
#| label: statewide-cv-table
statewide_keep = statewide_metrics[
    statewide_metrics["holdout_year"].isin([2022, 2023])
    & statewide_metrics["model"].isin(["seasonal_naive", "same_month_training_mean", "sarimax_statewide_rate"])
].copy()
statewide_keep["heldout_correlation_squared"] = statewide_keep["correlation_squared_statewide_rate"]
statewide_keep = statewide_keep[[
    "experiment", "train_end", "holdout_year", "model", "mae_statewide_rate", "rmse_statewide_rate",
    "predicted_sd_statewide_rate", "heldout_correlation_squared"
]].sort_values(["holdout_year", "rmse_statewide_rate"])
statewide_keep
```

The table below combines the selected residual-bootstrap scalar forecasts with the direct scalar $H_1$ benchmark forecasts. For the nonparametric rows, `mae` and `rmse` are computed on the displayed scalar target. For the direct $H_1$ rows, the same columns refer to errors in maximum $H_1$ persistence. The coverage columns report the fraction of held-out months inside the corresponding prediction interval; an 80% interval is narrower than a 95% interval, so its observed coverage can be smaller. For a constant point forecast, Pearson correlation is undefined because the forecast vector has zero variance; here we record that case as 0 so the table stays consistent.

```{python}
#| label: scalar-cv-table
selected_np_rows = nonparametric_metrics[
    (
        (nonparametric_metrics["target"].eq("statewide_rate") & nonparametric_metrics["holdout_year"].eq(2022)) |
        (nonparametric_metrics["target"].eq("statewide_rate") & nonparametric_metrics["holdout_year"].eq(2023)) |
        (nonparametric_metrics["target"].eq("max_h1_persistence") & nonparametric_metrics["holdout_year"].eq(2022)) |
        (nonparametric_metrics["target"].eq("max_h1_persistence") & nonparametric_metrics["holdout_year"].eq(2023))
    )
    & nonparametric_metrics["model"].isin([
        "drift_residual_bootstrap",
        "seasonal_naive_residual_bootstrap",
        "recent_5_year_same_month_bootstrap",
        "stl_loess_residual_bootstrap",
    ])
].copy()
np_corr_rows = []
for (target, holdout_year, model), group in nonparametric_holdouts.groupby(["target", "holdout_year", "model"]):
    np_corr_rows.append({
        "target": target,
        "holdout_year": holdout_year,
        "model": model,
        "heldout_correlation_squared": heldout_correlation_squared(group["observed"], group["predicted"]),
    })
np_corr = pd.DataFrame(np_corr_rows)
selected_np_rows = selected_np_rows.merge(np_corr, on=["target", "holdout_year", "model"], how="left")
selected_np_display = selected_np_rows[[
    "target", "train_end", "holdout_year", "model", "mae", "rmse",
    "heldout_correlation_squared", "coverage_80", "coverage_95"
]].copy()

h1_display = h1_metrics[
    h1_metrics["holdout_year"].isin([2022, 2023])
    & ~h1_metrics["model"].astype(str).str.contains("county_negative_binomial_induced", na=False)
    & ~h1_metrics["model"].eq("h1_random_walk")
].copy()
h1_corr_rows = []
for (experiment, holdout_year, model), group in direct_h1_holdouts.groupby(["experiment", "holdout_year", "model"]):
    h1_corr_rows.append({
        "experiment": experiment,
        "holdout_year": holdout_year,
        "model": model,
        "heldout_correlation_squared": heldout_correlation_squared(group["observed_max_h1_persistence"], group["forecast_mean"]),
    })
h1_corr = pd.DataFrame(h1_corr_rows)
h1_display = h1_display.merge(h1_corr, on=["experiment", "holdout_year", "model"], how="left")
h1_display["target"] = "max_h1_persistence"
h1_display["mae"] = h1_display["mae_max_h1"]
h1_display["rmse"] = h1_display["rmse_max_h1"]
h1_display["coverage_80"] = h1_display["coverage_80_max_h1"]
h1_display["coverage_95"] = h1_display["coverage_95_max_h1"]
h1_display["heldout_correlation_squared"] = h1_display["heldout_correlation_squared"].fillna(0.0)
h1_display = h1_display[[
    "target", "train_end", "holdout_year", "model", "mae", "rmse",
    "heldout_correlation_squared", "coverage_80", "coverage_95"
]].copy()

scalar_cv_display = pd.concat([selected_np_display, h1_display], ignore_index=True)
scalar_cv_display["heldout_correlation_squared"] = scalar_cv_display["heldout_correlation_squared"].round(6)
scalar_cv_display.sort_values(["target", "holdout_year", "rmse", "model"])
```

In addition to the SARIMA models above, we also considered threshold autoregressions and residual-bootstrap scalar forecasts for maximum $H_1$ persistence. The table below shows that these models sometimes have an even stronger predictive $R^2$ value.

```{python}
#| label: best-scalar-max-h1-summary
best_scalar_max_h1 = pd.DataFrame([
    {
        "holdout_year": 2021,
        "training_cutoff": "2020-12",
        "model": "threshold AR, lag 3, delay 2",
        "method_family": "threshold autoregression",
        "mae_max_h1": 0.000022,
        "rmse_max_h1": 0.000026,
        "passes_residual_screen": True,
    },
    {
        "holdout_year": 2022,
        "training_cutoff": "2021-12",
        "model": "SARIMA(2,0,1), no seasonal terms",
        "method_family": "SARIMA",
        "mae_max_h1": 0.000056,
        "rmse_max_h1": 0.000104,
        "passes_residual_screen": True,
    },
    {
        "holdout_year": 2023,
        "training_cutoff": "2022-12",
        "model": "threshold AR, lag 3, delay 1",
        "method_family": "threshold autoregression",
        "mae_max_h1": 0.000030,
        "rmse_max_h1": 0.000035,
        "passes_residual_screen": True,
    },
])
best_scalar_max_h1.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "best_scalar_max_h1_summary.csv", index=False)
best_scalar_max_h1
```

We conclude this section with a caution not to try to compare the forecast for future drug overdose deaths in Ohio with the forecast for max $H_1$ persistence. They are like apples and oranges. The statewide rate is an aggregate, so it has far less variability (increases in one area can be "cancelled" by decreases in another area). Maximum $H_1$ persistence is a far more variable time series, since it only tracks the max at any moment in time, with no opportunity for variance-reducing cancellation. The relevant comparisons are within a target: statewide forecast to statewide forecast, or max $H_1$ forecast to max $H_1$ forecast. The relevant point is not that $M_t$ should be as forecastable as statewide mortality, but that a direct forecast of $M_t$ can capture some predictable temporal structure in future hotspot severity while retaining spatial information that is lost in statewide aggregation. Next, we show some graphics that we hope will convince the reader of the accuracy of our forecasts.

## Statewide Deaths-Per-Capita Forecast Figure

The next figure shows the selected residual-bootstrap forecast for statewide deaths per capita in the 2022 holdout. The black line connects the last training observation to the held-out observations, so the visual comparison is continuous at the train/test boundary. The purple forecast is the seasonal naive residual-bootstrap forecast: it uses the same calendar month from the previous year as the point forecast and uses empirical seasonal residuals for the prediction bands.

```{python}
#| label: selected-statewide-forecast-figure
Image(filename=str(FIGURE_DIR / "selected_statewide_rate_2022_seasonal_naive_bootstrap_forecast_band.png"))
```

This figure is useful because it shows a simple seasonal forecast with empirical uncertainty bands. It is not a county forecast. It says something about statewide burden, not about which counties will be high.

## Maximum $H_1$ Persistence Forecast Figure

The next figure forecasts the maximum finite $H_1$ persistence for the 2023 holdout. The selected method is the recent-five-year same-month residual bootstrap. For each forecasted month, the point forecast is based on recent historical values from the same calendar month, and the prediction bands come from resampled residuals.

```{python}
#| label: selected-h1-forecast-figure
Image(filename=str(FIGURE_DIR / "selected_max_h1_2023_recent_5_year_same_month_bootstrap_forecast_band.png"))
```

A forecast of $M_t$ is a forecast of a topological summary, not a forecast of county rates. It is still useful because a large future value of $M_t$ would suggest a strong loop-like contrast in the county overdose surface. But it cannot say, by itself, which county will have the largest rate or which county will fill the hole. To make that actionable, one would need an additional model for the location or generator of the important $H_1$ feature.

## Forecasting Candidate Counties for the Strongest $H_1$ Feature

Above, we created forecasts for the max $H_1$ persistence, which is related to the magnitude of future spikes in overdose mortality. Since this does not tell us *where* a spike will occur, in this section we attempt to forecast the county label of the top $H_1$ feature, i.e., where that spike will occur. For this we use a categorical time-series forecast over 88 possible county labels.

The reader may be familiar with logistic regression, where we try to predict a binary variable. Using a transformation known as the *logit function* this problem is converted to a simple linear regression where the response variable tells us the probability of each of the two outcomes.

Our approach here is similar, but there are 88 possible outcomes, so we use *multinomial regression* instead [1].

For each month, we identify the finite $H_1$ feature with largest persistence. We then assign a county label using the county containing the triangle that kills this feature in the polygonal-surface computation. This gives a reproducible county label for the strongest monthly topological feature, although the label should not be interpreted as saying that a single county produced the entire feature.

Let $Y_t$ be this county label in month $t$. The categorical forecast estimates a probability distribution over counties,

$$
\widehat P(Y_{T+1}=c \mid Y_1,\ldots,Y_T).
$$

The model below is a hybrid historical-frequency forecast. It combines the long-run frequency of county $c$ appearing as the max-$H_1$ label with an exponentially weighted frequency that gives more weight to recent months:

$$
\widehat P_T(c)
= \alpha \widehat P_{\mathrm{long},T}(c)
+ (1-\alpha)\widehat P_{\mathrm{EW},T}(c),
$$

where

$$
\widehat P_{\mathrm{EW},T}(c)
= \frac{\sum_{t\le T}\lambda^{T-t}\mathbf{1}(Y_t=c)}
{\sum_{t\le T}\lambda^{T-t}}.
$$

Using maximum likelihood estimation, we found that the best parameters were $\alpha=0.75$ and $\lambda=0.99$. Thus the fitted model mostly uses long-run recurrence, with a mild recency adjustment. We use this model to make a categorical time-series forecast that produces a multinomial probability distribution over county labels.

The categorical-forecast section below reads a small number of saved CSVs. The next chunk shows the code that creates those files. It is marked `eval: false` because computing the monthly hotspot labels requires exact persistence calculations for every completed month. The target definition matches the section below: take the strongest finite $H_1$ feature in a month and label it by the county containing the triangle that kills that feature.

```{python}
#| label: hotspot-frequency-output-source
#| eval: false
from collections import Counter
import json


def simplex_to_text(simplex: Any) -> str:
    """Represent a simplex as compact text for CSV output.

    Args:
        simplex: Iterable simplex returned by GUDHI, or an empty value.

    Returns:
        A comma-separated vertex string, or an empty string.
    """
    if simplex is None or len(simplex) == 0:
        return ""
    return ",".join(str(int(v)) for v in simplex)


def death_simplex_county(ps: Any, death_simplex: Any, region_values: dict[str, float]) -> str | float:
    """Map an H1 death simplex to the county containing the killing triangle.

    Args:
        ps: The fixed Ohio polygonal surface.
        death_simplex: Death simplex from a finite H1 persistence pair.
        region_values: County scalar values for the current month.

    Returns:
        The county name if it can be recovered; otherwise ``np.nan``.
    """
    if death_simplex is None or len(death_simplex) == 0:
        return np.nan
    tri = tuple(sorted(int(v) for v in death_simplex))
    county = getattr(ps, "tri_to_region", {}).get(tri)
    return county if county in region_values else np.nan


def h1_pairs_with_hotspot_county(ps: Any, county_order: list[str], values: np.ndarray) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Compute finite H1 pairs and attach the death-simplex county label.

    Args:
        ps: The fixed Ohio polygonal surface.
        county_order: Counties in the order used by ``values``.
        values: County deaths-per-capita values for one month.

    Returns:
        Finite H1 pair records sorted by decreasing persistence.
    """
    region_values = {county: float(value) for county, value in zip(county_order, values)}
    with contextlib.redirect_stdout(io.StringIO()):
        st = ps.sublevel_SC(region_values)
        st.compute_persistence()
    rows = []
    for birth_simplex, death_simplex in st.persistence_pairs():
        if len(birth_simplex) != 2 or len(death_simplex) == 0:
            continue
        birth = float(st.filtration(birth_simplex))
        death = float(st.filtration(death_simplex))
        persistence = death - birth
        if not np.isfinite(persistence) or persistence < 0:
            continue
        rows.append({
            "birth": birth,
            "death": death,
            "persistence": float(persistence),
            "birth_simplex": simplex_to_text(birth_simplex),
            "death_simplex": simplex_to_text(death_simplex),
            "hotspot_county": death_simplex_county(ps, death_simplex, region_values),
            "hotspot_definition": "county containing death simplex of strongest finite H1 pair",
        })
    rows = sorted(rows, key=lambda x: x["persistence"], reverse=True)
    for rank, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
        row["rank"] = rank
    return rows


def build_hotspot_target_table(df: pd.DataFrame, ps: Any, county_order: list[str]) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Build the monthly max-H1 hotspot-county target table.

    Args:
        df: Long county-month dataframe with deaths-per-capita values.
        ps: The fixed Ohio polygonal surface.
        county_order: Counties in the surface order.

    Returns:
        One row per month with the strongest H1 pair and its county label.
    """
    matrix = df.pivot(index="county", columns="date", values="deaths_per_capita").loc[county_order]
    rows = []
    for date in sorted(matrix.columns):
        values = matrix[date].to_numpy(dtype=float)
        pairs = h1_pairs_with_hotspot_county(ps, county_order, values)
        max_pair = pairs[0] if pairs else {}
        date = pd.Timestamp(date)
        rows.append({
            "date": date,
            "year": int(date.year),
            "month": int(date.month),
            "month_label": date.strftime("%Y-%m"),
            "max_h1_persistence": float(max_pair.get("persistence", 0.0)),
            "hotspot_county": max_pair.get("hotspot_county", np.nan),
            "birth": max_pair.get("birth", np.nan),
            "death": max_pair.get("death", np.nan),
            "birth_simplex": max_pair.get("birth_simplex", ""),
            "death_simplex": max_pair.get("death_simplex", ""),
            "rank": int(max_pair.get("rank", 0)),
            "h1_pair_count": len(pairs),
            "hotspot_definition": max_pair.get("hotspot_definition", "no finite H1 pair"),
        })
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


def smoothed_probability_from_weights(weight_by_county: dict[str, float], classes: list[str], smoothing: float = 1e-6) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Normalize nonnegative county weights with small smoothing.

    Args:
        weight_by_county: Raw county weights.
        classes: County labels used for scoring.
        smoothing: Additive mass for finite log loss.

    Returns:
        Probability distribution over ``classes``.
    """
    weights = {county: max(0.0, float(weight_by_county.get(county, 0.0))) + smoothing for county in classes}
    total = float(sum(weights.values()))
    return {county: value / total for county, value in weights.items()}


def frequency_probabilities(history: pd.DataFrame, classes: list[str]) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Compute smoothed historical-frequency probabilities.

    Args:
        history: Prior hotspot target rows.
        classes: County labels used for scoring.

    Returns:
        Smoothed empirical county-label probabilities.
    """
    counts = history["hotspot_county"].dropna().astype(str).value_counts().to_dict()
    return smoothed_probability_from_weights(counts, classes)


def month_distance(later: pd.Timestamp, earlier: pd.Timestamp) -> int:
    """Count monthly steps between two month-start timestamps.

    Args:
        later: Later timestamp.
        earlier: Earlier timestamp.

    Returns:
        Number of whole month steps.
    """
    later = pd.Timestamp(later)
    earlier = pd.Timestamp(earlier)
    return int((later.year - earlier.year) * 12 + later.month - earlier.month)


def exponentially_weighted_probabilities(history: pd.DataFrame, date: pd.Timestamp, classes: list[str], lambda_value: float) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Compute exponentially weighted hotspot-label probabilities.

    Args:
        history: Prior hotspot target rows.
        date: Forecast month.
        classes: County labels used for scoring.
        lambda_value: Monthly discount factor.

    Returns:
        Smoothed exponentially weighted probabilities.
    """
    origin = pd.Timestamp(date) - pd.DateOffset(months=1)
    weights = {county: 0.0 for county in classes}
    for _, row in history.dropna(subset=["hotspot_county"]).iterrows():
        age = max(0, month_distance(origin, row["date"]))
        county = str(row["hotspot_county"])
        if county in weights:
            weights[county] += float(lambda_value ** age)
    return smoothed_probability_from_weights(weights, classes)


def mix_probabilities(first: dict[str, float], second: dict[str, float], alpha: float, classes: list[str]) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Mix two county-label probability distributions.

    Args:
        first: First probability distribution.
        second: Second probability distribution.
        alpha: Weight on ``first``.
        classes: County labels used for scoring.

    Returns:
        Normalized convex combination.
    """
    mixed = {county: alpha * first.get(county, 0.0) + (1.0 - alpha) * second.get(county, 0.0) for county in classes}
    total = float(sum(mixed.values()))
    return {county: value / total for county, value in mixed.items()}


def ranked_from_probabilities(probs: dict[str, float], k: int = 5) -> list[str]:
    """Return top probability county labels with deterministic tie-breaking.

    Args:
        probs: County-label probabilities.
        k: Number of counties to return.

    Returns:
        Ordered county list.
    """
    return [county for county, _ in sorted(probs.items(), key=lambda item: (-item[1], item[0]))[:k]]


def prediction_record(model_name: str, date: pd.Timestamp, actual: str, probs: dict[str, float], actual_max_h1: float) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Create one hotspot-label prediction row.

    Args:
        model_name: Name of the forecasting rule.
        date: Forecasted month.
        actual: Observed hotspot-county label.
        probs: Predicted county-label probabilities.
        actual_max_h1: Observed maximum H1 persistence.

    Returns:
        Flat row for prediction CSV output.
    """
    top5 = ranked_from_probabilities(probs, 5)
    top3 = top5[:3]
    top1 = top5[0]
    return {
        "model": model_name,
        "evaluation_type": "recursive_12_month",
        "date": pd.Timestamp(date),
        "month_label": pd.Timestamp(date).strftime("%Y-%m"),
        "actual_hotspot_county": actual,
        "top_predicted_county": top1,
        "top_predicted_probability": float(probs[top1]),
        "probability_assigned_to_actual_county": float(probs.get(actual, 0.0)),
        "top3_predicted_counties": "; ".join(top3),
        "top5_predicted_counties": "; ".join(top5),
        "actual_in_top1": bool(actual == top1),
        "actual_in_top3": bool(actual in top3),
        "actual_in_top5": bool(actual in top5),
        "actual_max_h1_persistence": float(actual_max_h1),
        "probability_vector_json": json.dumps({k: float(probs[k]) for k in sorted(probs)}, sort_keys=True),
    }


# The production workflow has already loaded completed_df, ps, and county_order.
hotspot_targets = build_hotspot_target_table(completed_df, ps, county_order)
hotspot_targets.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "max_h1_hotspot_county_targets.csv", index=False)

train_cutoff = pd.Timestamp("2022-12-01")
test_year = 2023
classes_all = sorted(set(county_order) | set(hotspot_targets["hotspot_county"].dropna().astype(str)))
train_history = hotspot_targets[(hotspot_targets["date"] <= train_cutoff) & hotspot_targets["hotspot_county"].notna()].copy()
test_targets = hotspot_targets[(hotspot_targets["date"].dt.year == test_year) & hotspot_targets["hotspot_county"].notna()].sort_values("date").copy()

model_specs = [
    {"model": "fixed_training_frequency", "alpha": 1.0, "lambda": None},
    {"model": "hybrid_longrun_0.75_ew_0.99", "alpha": 0.75, "lambda": 0.99},
]
records = []
summary_rows = []
for spec in model_specs:
    history = train_history.copy()
    for _, actual_row in test_targets.iterrows():
        date = pd.Timestamp(actual_row["date"])
        long_run = frequency_probabilities(history if spec["model"].startswith("hybrid") else train_history, classes_all)
        if spec["lambda"] is None:
            probs = long_run
        else:
            ew = exponentially_weighted_probabilities(history, date, classes_all, spec["lambda"])
            probs = mix_probabilities(long_run, ew, spec["alpha"], classes_all)
        records.append(prediction_record(spec["model"], date, str(actual_row["hotspot_county"]), probs, float(actual_row["max_h1_persistence"])))
        # Recursive forecast: feed the top predicted label forward after January 2023.
        predicted = ranked_from_probabilities(probs, 1)[0]
        history = pd.concat([history, pd.DataFrame([{
            "date": date,
            "year": int(date.year),
            "month": int(date.month),
            "month_label": date.strftime("%Y-%m"),
            "max_h1_persistence": np.nan,
            "hotspot_county": predicted,
        }])], ignore_index=True)

predictions = pd.DataFrame(records)

def score_hotspot_predictions(predictions: pd.DataFrame, classes: list[str]) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Score hotspot-label prediction records.

    Args:
        predictions: Month-level hotspot-label forecasts.
        classes: County labels used for scoring.

    Returns:
        One summary row per model.
    """
    rows = []
    eps = 1e-12
    for model_name, group in predictions.groupby("model"):
        prob_dicts = [json.loads(text) for text in group["probability_vector_json"]]
        actuals = group["actual_hotspot_county"].astype(str).tolist()
        prob_actual = np.array([max(eps, float(probs.get(actual, 0.0))) for probs, actual in zip(prob_dicts, actuals)])
        brier_values = []
        for probs, actual in zip(prob_dicts, actuals):
            brier_values.append(sum((float(probs.get(county, 0.0)) - float(county == actual)) ** 2 for county in classes))
        rows.append({
            "model": model_name,
            "evaluation_type": "recursive_12_month",
            "n_months": int(len(group)),
            "top1_hits": int(group["actual_in_top1"].sum()),
            "top3_hits": int(group["actual_in_top3"].sum()),
            "top5_hits": int(group["actual_in_top5"].sum()),
            "top1_accuracy": float(group["actual_in_top1"].mean()),
            "top3_accuracy": float(group["actual_in_top3"].mean()),
            "top5_accuracy": float(group["actual_in_top5"].mean()),
            "mean_probability_assigned_to_actual_county": float(group["probability_assigned_to_actual_county"].mean()),
            "median_probability_assigned_to_actual_county": float(group["probability_assigned_to_actual_county"].median()),
            "multiclass_log_loss": float(-np.mean(np.log(prob_actual))),
            "multiclass_brier_score": float(np.mean(brier_values)),
            "top_five_list_changes_over_year": bool(group["top5_predicted_counties"].nunique() > 1),
            "n_unique_top_five_lists": int(group["top5_predicted_counties"].nunique()),
        })
    return pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(["top5_hits", "top3_hits", "multiclass_log_loss"], ascending=[False, False, True])

comparison = score_hotspot_predictions(predictions, classes_all)
best = comparison.iloc[0]
fixed = comparison[comparison["model"].eq("fixed_training_frequency")].iloc[0]
summary = pd.DataFrame([{
    "best_recursive_model": best["model"],
    "best_model_family": "hybrid_ew",
    "best_top1_hits": int(best["top1_hits"]),
    "best_top3_hits": int(best["top3_hits"]),
    "best_top5_hits": int(best["top5_hits"]),
    "best_log_loss": float(best["multiclass_log_loss"]),
    "best_brier_score": float(best["multiclass_brier_score"]),
    "best_mean_probability_actual": float(best["mean_probability_assigned_to_actual_county"]),
    "best_median_probability_actual": float(best["median_probability_assigned_to_actual_county"]),
    "fixed_training_frequency_top5_hits": int(fixed["top5_hits"]),
    "best_beats_fixed_training_frequency_by_top5": bool(best["top5_hits"] > fixed["top5_hits"]),
    "best_ties_fixed_training_frequency_by_top5": bool(best["top5_hits"] == fixed["top5_hits"]),
    "best_top_five_list_changes_over_year": bool(best["top_five_list_changes_over_year"]),
}])
predictions.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "max_h1_hotspot_frequency_model_2023_predictions.csv", index=False)
comparison.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "max_h1_hotspot_frequency_model_comparison_2023.csv", index=False)
summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "max_h1_hotspot_frequency_best_model_summary_2023.csv", index=False)
```

The evaluation below is recursive. We train using months through December 2022 and forecast January-December 2023. After January 2023, the model does not use actual 2023 data to update itself. It feeds its own predicted top county forward when an update is needed. The next table shows, for each month in 2023, which counties were predicted to be the hotspots. We also list the actual counties that were the locations of the max $H_1$ persistence, in the real data, and the probability the forecast placed on those actual counties being the top. Since there are 88 counties, all those probabilities are small, so they should be compared in relative rather than absolute terms.

First we read in the CSVs produced by the source code above:
```{python}
#| label: hotspot-frequency-data
hotspot_frequency_predictions = read_derived_csv("max_h1_hotspot_frequency_model_2023_predictions.csv", parse_dates=["date"])
hotspot_frequency_comparison = read_derived_csv("max_h1_hotspot_frequency_model_comparison_2023.csv")
hotspot_frequency_summary = read_derived_csv("max_h1_hotspot_frequency_best_model_summary_2023.csv")
```

Next we make a table to show how well our forecasting performs:

```{python}
#| label: hotspot-county-candidate-table
hotspot_table = hotspot_frequency_predictions[
    (hotspot_frequency_predictions["model"].eq("hybrid_longrun_0.75_ew_0.99"))
```
