```{python}
    & (hotspot_frequency_predictions["evaluation_type"].eq("recursive_12_month"))
].copy()
hotspot_table = hotspot_table[[
    "month_label",
    "actual_hotspot_county",
    "actual_max_h1_persistence",
    "top5_predicted_counties",
    "actual_in_top5",
    "probability_assigned_to_actual_county",
]].rename(columns={
    "month_label": "month",
    "actual_hotspot_county": "actual county label",
    "actual_max_h1_persistence": "actual max H1 persistence",
    "top5_predicted_counties": "top five predicted counties",
    "actual_in_top5": "actual county in top five",
    "probability_assigned_to_actual_county": "probability on actual county",
})
hotspot_table.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "hotspot_candidate_list_2023_table.csv", index=False)
hotspot_table
```

Because of vine switching, we did not focus solely on predicting the top county. Instead, we asked the model to output the top five county predictions in each month. In 8 of 12 months, the true top county was on that list. Public health workers could use this model (or an improved one involving covariates like economic information, demographics, or drug supply data) to decide where to stage resources so that they could be ready for the next spike.

## Interpretation of Topological Forecasts

Sublevel set $H_1$ persistence is best read as a contrast in the county surface. Low-rate counties enter the filtration first. If those low-rate counties form a loop around counties that have not yet entered, an $H_1$ feature is born. The feature dies when the threshold becomes high enough that the hole fills in. The persistence is the difference between those two threshold values.

The persistence value is not, in general, a full county-level forecast. Even if one feature is associated with a striking local contrast, knowing only the maximum persistence does not tell us the future rate in any particular county. It tells us the difference to the next highest county. Still, this can be helpful to public health departments, so that they can consider "what if" analyses, or look at a whole region to know if their resources are sufficient for that region.


The following non-executed source chunk records the remaining saved-output provenance: future-forecast summaries retained for comparison, direct-versus-induced $H_1$ comparison files, source manifests, validation checks, and the saved figure manifest.

```{python}
#| label: forecasting-output-manifest-source
#| eval: false
# ---- Future forecast code retained for provenance: fit through 2023, simulate future county fields, and save future summaries ----

nonparametric_forecasts = []
nonparametric_metrics = []
for target in ["statewide_rate", "max_h1_persistence"]:
    for train_end, holdout_year in [(pd.Timestamp("2021-12-01"), 2022), (pd.Timestamp("2022-12-01"), 2023)]:
        forecasts, metrics = run_nonparametric_holdout(target, train_end, holdout_year, n_bootstrap=3000)
        nonparametric_forecasts.append(forecasts)
        nonparametric_metrics.append(metrics)
        plot_nonparametric_best_forecast(forecasts, metrics, target, holdout_year)
        plot_nonparametric_model_comparison(forecasts, target, holdout_year)

nonparametric_forecasts = pd.concat(nonparametric_forecasts, ignore_index=True)
nonparametric_metrics = pd.concat(nonparametric_metrics, ignore_index=True)
nonparametric_forecasts.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "nonparametric_scalar_forecast_holdouts.csv", index=False)
nonparametric_metrics.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "nonparametric_scalar_forecast_cv_metrics.csv", index=False)
nonparametric_best_models = (
    nonparametric_metrics.sort_values(["target", "holdout_year", "rmse", "mae"], ascending=[True, True, True, True])
    .groupby(["target", "holdout_year"], as_index=False)
    .head(1)
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
nonparametric_best_models.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "nonparametric_scalar_best_models_by_holdout.csv", index=False)
nonparametric_best_models

# ---- Future plots: statewide burden, expected exceeding counties, top county probabilities, future H1, and ranked H1 heatmap ----

def plot_selected_nonparametric_forecast(
    forecasts: pd.DataFrame,
    target: str,
    holdout_year: int,
    model_name: str,
    line_color: str,
    stem: str,
) -> None:
    """Save one selected residual-bootstrap forecast-band figure.

    Args:
        See the function signature for input names and types.

    Returns:
        The dataframe, series, array, model object, or scalar described by the function name.
    """
    plot_df = forecasts[
        forecasts["target"].eq(target)
        & forecasts["holdout_year"].eq(holdout_year)
        & forecasts["model"].eq(model_name)
    ].sort_values("date")
    if plot_df.empty:
        raise ValueError(f"Missing forecast rows for {target}, {holdout_year}, {model_name}")
    train_end = pd.Timestamp(plot_df["train_end"].iloc[0])
    y = target_series(target)
    history = y[(y.index >= train_end - pd.DateOffset(years=5)) & (y.index <= train_end)]
    observed_holdout = pd.Series(plot_df["observed"].to_numpy(dtype=float), index=pd.to_datetime(plot_df["date"]))
    observed_line = pd.concat([history.tail(1), observed_holdout])
    label = NONPARAMETRIC_TARGETS[target]["label"]
    ylabel = NONPARAMETRIC_TARGETS[target]["ylabel"]
    readable_model = model_name.replace("_", " ")

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.8))
    ax.plot(history.index, history.values, color="#111111", lw=1.35, alpha=0.65, label="Training history")
    ax.plot(observed_line.index, observed_line.values, color="#111111", marker="o", lw=1.8, label="Observed through holdout")
    ax.plot(plot_df["date"], plot_df["predicted"], color=line_color, marker="o", lw=1.9, label=f"Forecast: {readable_model}")
    ax.fill_between(plot_df["date"], plot_df["lower_80"], plot_df["upper_80"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.18, label="80% bootstrap interval")
    ax.fill_between(plot_df["date"], plot_df["lower_90"], plot_df["upper_90"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.09, label="90% bootstrap interval")
    ax.axvline(train_end, color="#666666", lw=1, ls=":")
    ax.set_title(f"{label}, {holdout_year} holdout\nSelected forecast: {readable_model}")
    ax.set_xlabel("Month")
    ax.set_ylabel(ylabel)
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    fig.tight_layout()
    for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
        fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"{stem}.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()


plot_selected_nonparametric_forecast(
    nonparametric_forecasts,
    target="statewide_rate",
    holdout_year=2022,
    model_name="seasonal_naive_residual_bootstrap",
    line_color="#6a4c93",
    stem="selected_statewide_rate_2022_seasonal_naive_bootstrap_forecast_band",
)
plot_selected_nonparametric_forecast(
    nonparametric_forecasts,
    target="max_h1_persistence",
    holdout_year=2023,
    model_name="recent_5_year_same_month_bootstrap",
    line_color="#8b0000",
    stem="selected_max_h1_2023_recent_5_year_same_month_bootstrap_forecast_band",
)

# ---- Direct versus model-induced H1 comparison plot and CSV ----

print("Best models used in the forecast-band figures")
display(nonparametric_best_models)
print("All scalar nonparametric forecast metrics")
display(nonparametric_metrics.sort_values(["target", "holdout_year", "rmse", "mae"], ascending=[True, True, True, True]))

# ---- Model comparison and validation/source manifests ----

future_train = df_all[df_all["date"] <= CONFIG["completed_training_end"]].copy()
future_hotspot_threshold = choose_hotspot_threshold(future_train)
future_fit = fit_count_model(future_train)
future_sims = simulate_paths(
    future_train,
    future_fit,
    CONFIG["future_horizon_months"],
    CONFIG["n_future_simulations"],
    np.random.default_rng(CONFIG["random_seed"] + 9000),
)
future_county, future_expected_counts = summarize_county_simulations(future_sims, future_hotspot_threshold)
future_state = summarize_statewide_from_county_sims(future_sims)
future_sim_h1, future_ranked_h1, future_h1_failures = compute_simulated_h1(future_sims, ps, county_order, CONFIG["top_rank_count"], "future_train_through_2023")
future_h1_summary, future_h1_ranked_summary = summarize_h1_simulations(future_sim_h1, future_ranked_h1, h1_threshold)
future_direct_h1 = direct_h1_scalar_forecast(observed_h1[observed_h1["date"] <= CONFIG["completed_training_end"]].copy(), sorted(future_state["date"].unique()), h1_threshold)

future_state.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "future_statewide_forecast.csv", index=False)
future_county.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "future_county_exceedance_probabilities.csv", index=False)
future_expected_counts.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "future_expected_exceeding_counties_by_horizon.csv", index=False)
future_h1_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "future_h1_model_induced_forecast.csv", index=False)
future_h1_ranked_summary.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "future_h1_ranked_persistence_forecast.csv", index=False)
future_direct_h1.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "direct_h1_forecast_summary.csv", index=False)
future_h1_failures.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "future_h1_failures.csv", index=False)

future_model_summary = pd.DataFrame([{
    "fit_label": "future_train_through_2023_12",
    "response": "county-month overdose death count",
    "distribution": "negative binomial",
    "exposure_offset": "log county population",
    "county_effects": "county fixed effects",
    "seasonality": "month-of-year fixed effects",
    "time_trend": "linear monthly time index",
    "lag_terms": "log(1 + county lag deaths), lagged county deaths per capita, log(1 + statewide lag deaths)",
    "method": future_fit.method,
    "formula": future_fit.formula,
    "alpha": future_fit.alpha,
    "training_rows": future_fit.n_train_rows,
    "training_months": future_fit.n_train_months,
    "train_start": future_fit.train_start.date().isoformat(),
    "train_end": future_fit.train_end.date().isoformat(),
    "forecast_horizon_months": CONFIG["future_horizon_months"],
    "simulations": CONFIG["n_future_simulations"],
    "hotspot_threshold": future_hotspot_threshold,
    "converged": future_fit.converged,
    "aic": float(getattr(future_fit.result, "aic", np.nan)),
}])
model_summaries = pd.concat([model_summaries, future_model_summary], ignore_index=True)
model_summaries.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "forecast_model_summary.csv", index=False)

display(future_county.sort_values(["horizon", "exceedance_probability"], ascending=[True, False]).groupby("horizon").head(5))
display(future_h1_summary)

# ---- Direct versus model-induced H1 comparison and saved comparison plot ----

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.8))
hist = statewide_series(future_train)
ax.plot(hist["date"], hist["statewide_rate"], color="#111111", lw=1.5, label="Observed through 2023")
ax.plot(future_state["date"], future_state["predicted_median_rate"], color="#2a9d8f", marker="o", lw=1.7, label="Forecast median")
ax.fill_between(future_state["date"], future_state["statewide_rate_lower_80"], future_state["statewide_rate_upper_80"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.18, label="80% interval")
ax.fill_between(future_state["date"], future_state["statewide_rate_lower_95"], future_state["statewide_rate_upper_95"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.09, label="95% interval")
ax.set_title("Future statewide overdose rate forecast trained through December 2023")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("Deaths per capita")
ax.legend(fontsize=8)
fig.tight_layout()
for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
    fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"future_statewide_rate_forecast_band.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4.8))
ax.plot(future_expected_counts["horizon"], future_expected_counts["median_exceeding_counties"], marker="o", color="#264653", label="Median")
ax.fill_between(future_expected_counts["horizon"], future_expected_counts["lower_80"], future_expected_counts["upper_80"], color="#e9c46a", alpha=0.35, label="80% interval")
ax.fill_between(future_expected_counts["horizon"], future_expected_counts["lower_95"], future_expected_counts["upper_95"], color="#f4a261", alpha=0.18, label="95% interval")
ax.set_title("Forecast number of counties above the hotspot threshold")
ax.set_xlabel("Forecast horizon in months")
ax.set_ylabel("County count")
ax.legend()
fig.tight_layout()
for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
    fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"future_expected_exceeding_counties_by_horizon.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

for horizon in [1, 3, 6, 12]:
    top = future_county[future_county["horizon"].eq(horizon)].sort_values("exceedance_probability", ascending=False).head(15).sort_values("exceedance_probability")
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5.8))
    ax.barh(top["county"], top["exceedance_probability"], color="#457b9d")
    ax.set_xlim(0, max(0.05, top["exceedance_probability"].max() * 1.15))
    ax.set_title(f"Largest county hotspot probabilities, h = {horizon} months")
    ax.set_xlabel("Forecast probability")
    fig.tight_layout()
    for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
        fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"future_top_county_exceedance_probabilities_h{horizon}.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.8))
ax.plot(observed_h1["date"], observed_h1["max_h1_persistence"], color="#111111", lw=1.4, label="Observed through 2023")
ax.plot(future_h1_summary["date"], future_h1_summary["max_h1_median"], color="#2a9d8f", marker="o", lw=1.7, label="Model-induced median")
ax.fill_between(future_h1_summary["date"], future_h1_summary["max_h1_lower_80"], future_h1_summary["max_h1_upper_80"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.18, label="80% interval")
ax.fill_between(future_h1_summary["date"], future_h1_summary["max_h1_lower_95"], future_h1_summary["max_h1_upper_95"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.09, label="95% interval")
ax.axhline(h1_threshold, color="#a23b72", ls=":", lw=1.1, label="Reference H1 threshold")
ax.set_title("Future model-induced maximum H1 persistence forecast")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("Max finite H1 persistence")
ax.legend(fontsize=8)
fig.tight_layout()
for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
    fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"future_h1_max_persistence_forecast_band.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

heat = future_h1_ranked_summary.pivot(index="rank", columns="horizon", values="persistence_median").sort_index()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5.5))
im = ax.imshow(heat.to_numpy(), aspect="auto", cmap="viridis")
ax.set_xticks(range(len(heat.columns)), heat.columns)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(heat.index)), heat.index)
ax.set_xlabel("Forecast horizon in months")
ax.set_ylabel("H1 persistence rank")
ax.set_title("Median forecast persistence by rank and horizon")
fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="Median persistence")
fig.tight_layout()
for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
    fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"h1_ranked_persistence_forecast_heatmap.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

# ---- Forecast model comparison summary and recommended-model CSV outputs ----

best_direct = future_direct_h1[future_direct_h1["model"].eq("h1_sarimax_scalar")].copy()
if best_direct["forecast_mean"].isna().all():
    best_direct = future_direct_h1[future_direct_h1["model"].eq("h1_recent_12_month_average")].copy()
comparison = future_h1_summary.merge(
    best_direct[["horizon", "date", "model", "forecast_mean", "lower_80", "upper_80", "lower_95", "upper_95", "prob_exceeds_h1_threshold"]],
    on=["horizon", "date"],
    how="left",
).rename(columns={
    "max_h1_median": "model_induced_median",
    "max_h1_lower_80": "model_induced_lower_80",
    "max_h1_upper_80": "model_induced_upper_80",
    "max_h1_lower_95": "model_induced_lower_95",
    "max_h1_upper_95": "model_induced_upper_95",
    "model": "direct_h1_model",
    "forecast_mean": "direct_forecast_mean",
    "lower_80": "direct_lower_80",
    "upper_80": "direct_upper_80",
    "lower_95": "direct_lower_95",
    "upper_95": "direct_upper_95",
})
comparison.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "direct_vs_model_induced_h1_forecast_comparison.csv", index=False)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4.8))
ax.plot(comparison["horizon"], comparison["model_induced_median"], marker="o", color="#2a9d8f", label="Model-induced median")
ax.fill_between(comparison["horizon"], comparison["model_induced_lower_80"], comparison["model_induced_upper_80"], color="#2a9d8f", alpha=0.18, label="Model-induced 80%")
ax.plot(comparison["horizon"], comparison["direct_forecast_mean"], marker="s", color="#a23b72", ls="--", label="Direct scalar forecast")
ax.fill_between(comparison["horizon"], comparison["direct_lower_80"], comparison["direct_upper_80"], color="#a23b72", alpha=0.12, label="Direct 80%")
ax.set_title("Direct scalar H1 forecast and model-induced H1 forecast")
ax.set_xlabel("Forecast horizon in months")
ax.set_ylabel("Max finite H1 persistence")
ax.legend(fontsize=8)
fig.tight_layout()
for ext in ["png", "pdf"]:
    fig.savefig(FIGURE_DIR / f"direct_vs_model_induced_h1_forecast_comparison.{ext}", dpi=180 if ext == "png" else None, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
comparison

model_comparison = statewide_metrics.sort_values(["holdout_year", "rmse_statewide_rate"]).copy()
recommended = []
for year, group in model_comparison.groupby("holdout_year"):
    best = group.iloc[0]
    recommended.append({"holdout_year": year, "best_statewide_rate_model_by_rmse": best["model"], "best_statewide_rate_rmse": best["rmse_statewide_rate"]})
recommended_df = pd.DataFrame(recommended)
model_comparison.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "forecast_model_comparison_summary.csv", index=False)
recommended_df.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "forecast_recommended_models_by_holdout.csv", index=False)
display_columns = [
    "experiment",
    "train_end",
    "holdout_year",
    "model",
    "mae_statewide_rate",
    "rmse_statewide_rate",
    "predicted_sd_statewide_rate",
    "correlation_squared_defined",
]
display(model_comparison[display_columns])
display(recommended_df)

# ---- Additional production source block ----

figure_files = sorted([p for p in FIGURE_DIR.iterdir() if p.suffix.lower() in {".png", ".pdf"}])
csv_files = sorted([p for p in OUTPUT_DIR.iterdir() if p.suffix.lower() == ".csv"])

sources_used = pd.DataFrame([
    {"source_type": "input", "path": str(CONFIG["counts_wide_path"].relative_to(HERE)), "description": "county-month overdose death counts"},
    {"source_type": "input", "path": str(CONFIG["rates_wide_path"].relative_to(HERE)), "description": "county-month deaths per capita file used for consistency checks and population fallback"},
    {"source_type": "input", "path": str(CONFIG["population_wide_path"].relative_to(HERE)), "description": "annual county population denominators"},
    {"source_type": "input", "path": str(CONFIG["adjacency_path"].relative_to(HERE)), "description": "Ohio county adjacency file used to build the fixed complex"},
    {"source_type": "input", "path": str(CONFIG["bootstrap_significance_path"].relative_to(HERE)), "description": "bootstrap H1 band used only as a reference threshold when available"},
    {"source_type": "code", "path": "forecasting-vineyards.qmd", "description": "forecasting notebook source chunks for derived forecasting outputs"},
    {"source_type": "code", "path": "vineyard/polygonal_surface.py", "description": "local exact Sublevel set persistence helper"},
    {"source_type": "reference", "path": "https://otexts.com/fpp3/", "description": "Forecasting: Principles and Practice, third edition; simple methods, residual diagnostics, and bootstrap forecast intervals"},
])
sources_used.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "forecast_sources_used.csv", index=False)

validation_checks = pd.DataFrame([
    {"check": "number of counties", "value": df_all["county"].nunique(), "passed": df_all["county"].nunique() == 88},
    {"check": "number of observed months", "value": df_all["date"].nunique(), "passed": df_all["date"].nunique() == 213},
    {"check": "all modeled rows have population", "value": int(df_all["population"].isna().sum()), "passed": not df_all["population"].isna().any()},
    {"check": "2024 excluded from main model selection", "value": preliminary_df["date"].nunique(), "passed": preliminary_df["date"].nunique() > 0},
    {"check": "holdout experiments completed", "value": len(holdout_outputs), "passed": len(holdout_outputs) == len(CONFIG["holdouts"])},
    {"check": "count model convergence for holdouts", "value": bool(model_summaries.loc[model_summaries["fit_label"].str.contains("train_through"), "converged"].all()), "passed": bool(model_summaries.loc[model_summaries["fit_label"].str.contains("train_through"), "converged"].all())},
    {"check": "future forecast generated for 88 counties and 12 horizons", "value": len(future_county), "passed": len(future_county) == 88 * CONFIG["future_horizon_months"]},
    {"check": "future H1 computations succeeded", "value": len(future_h1_failures), "passed": len(future_h1_failures) == 0},
    {"check": "CSV outputs saved", "value": len(csv_files), "passed": len(csv_files) >= 18},
    {"check": "plot files saved", "value": len(figure_files), "passed": len(figure_files) >= 20},
])
validation_checks.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "forecast_validation_checks.csv", index=False)
figure_manifest = pd.DataFrame([{"figure": str(p.relative_to(OUTPUT_DIR)), "path": str(p)} for p in figure_files])
figure_manifest.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "forecast_figure_manifest.csv", index=False)

summary_table = pd.DataFrame([
    {"item": "data_date_range", "value": f"{df_all['date'].min().date()} to {df_all['date'].max().date()}"},
    {"item": "excluded_preliminary_months", "value": f"{preliminary_df['date'].min().date()} to {preliminary_df['date'].max().date()}" if len(preliminary_df) else "none"},
    {"item": "training_holdout_splits", "value": "; ".join([f"{s['experiment']}: train through {s['train_end'].date()}, forecast {s['holdout_start'].year}" for s in CONFIG["holdouts"]])},
    {"item": "models_tried", "value": "seasonal naive, last observation, recent 12-month average, same-month historical mean, statewide SARIMAX, county negative-binomial, direct H1 scalar models, nonparametric residual-bootstrap scalar forecasts, STL/LOESS residual-bootstrap scalar forecasts"},
    {"item": "holdout_simulations", "value": CONFIG["n_holdout_simulations"]},
    {"item": "future_simulations", "value": CONFIG["n_future_simulations"]},
    {"item": "recommended_county_model", "value": "county negative-binomial for county exceedance and model-induced H1, because it forecasts the county surface"},
    {"item": "warnings", "value": "2024 observed months are treated as preliminary and excluded from main training/validation; zero-heavy county counts may motivate a future zero-inflated comparison."},
])
summary_table.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / "forecast_rebuild_final_summary.csv", index=False)

display(validation_checks)
display(summary_table)
print(f"Saved {len(csv_files)} CSV files to {OUTPUT_DIR}")
print(f"Saved {len(figure_files)} figure files to {FIGURE_DIR}")
```


## Limitations and Future Work

Forecasting all county values is stronger than forecasting one topological summary, because the topology can be computed from the simulated county surface. But, as we saw above, our county negative-binomial model was not trustworthy. The 2023 decline in overdose deaths is a reminder that overdose dynamics can change faster than a simple univariate forecasting rule expects. That's why experts in this field include covariates in their models, with information about drug supply indicators, demographic structure, treatment access, reporting delays, policy, etc. We omitted those covariates to keep this file simple, as the main goal was to show how to forecast future max $H_1$ persistence, and how to report the resulting information to local health departments in a meaningful way.

The statewide deaths-per-capita forecast was easier but ignores county heterogeneity. The maximum $H_1$ forecast keeps one topology-aware feature of the county surface but loses most of the county-level information. The categorical candidate-list forecast pairs the size of the strongest $H_1$ feature with a county label associated with that feature. It is useful as a proof of concept, but a serious operational version should include covariates and a stronger model for local mortality dynamics.

The main take-away is that when a reliable county-level forecast is not yet available, forecasting vines like the max $H_1$ persistence (plus which county this occurs in) is more tractable and gives useful information about future spikes and hotspots. We plan to return to the question of forecasting future (county, month) death counts using more sophisticated models, in future work.

## References

[1] Anderson, F. *Logistic and Multinomial Regressions by Example: Hands on approach using R*, CreateSpace Independent Publishing Platform, 2016.

[2] Choudhuri, S., Basu, K., Thomas, K., and Sen, A. (2019). Predicting future opioid incidences today. *arXiv:1906.08891*. https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.08891

[3] Cohen-Steiner, D., Edelsbrunner, H., and Morozov, D. (2006). Vines and vineyards by updating persistence in linear time. *Proceedings of the Twenty-second Annual Symposium on Computational Geometry*, 119-126. https://doi.org/10.1145/1137856.1137877

[4] Cohen-Steiner, D., Edelsbrunner, H., and Harer, J. (2007). Stability of persistence diagrams. *Discrete & Computational Geometry*, 37, 103-120.

[5] Edelsbrunner, H. and Harer, J. (2010). *Computational Topology: An Introduction*. American Mathematical Society.

[6] Ertugrul, A. M., Lin, Y.-R., and Taskaya-Temizel, T. (2020). CASTNet: Community-attentive spatio-temporal networks for opioid overdose forecasting. In Machine Learning and Knowledge Discovery in
Databases, pp. 432–448. Springer, 2020. doi: 10.1007/978-3-030
-46133-1_26

[7] Gidea, M. and Katz, Y. (2018). Topological data analysis of financial time series: Landscapes of crashes. *Physica A*, 491, 820-834.

[8] Hepler, S. A., McKnight, E., Bonny, A. E., and Kline, D. M. (2019). A latent spatial factor approach for synthesizing opioid-associated deaths and treatment admissions in Ohio counties. *Epidemiology*, 30(3), 365-370.

[9] Hickok, A., Needell, D., and Porter, M. A. (2022). Analysis of spatial and spatiotemporal anomalies using persistent homology: Case studies with COVID-19 data. *SIAM Journal on Mathematics of Data Science*, 4(3), 1116-1144. https://doi.org/10.1137/21M1435033

[10] Hilbe, J. M. (2011). *Negative Binomial Regression*. Cambridge University Press.

[11] Hyndman, R. J. and Athanasopoulos, G. (2021). *Forecasting: Principles and Practice*, 3rd edition. https://otexts.com/fpp3/

[12] Kline, D. M., Ji, C., and Hepler, S. A. (2021). A multivariate spatio-temporal model of the opioid epidemic in Ohio: a factor model approach. *Health Services and Outcomes Research Methodology*, 21(1), 42-53.

[13] Liu, X., Carter, J., Ray, B., and Mohler, G. (2020). Point process modeling of drug overdoses with heterogeneous and missing data. Annals of Applied Statistics, volume 15, issue 1, DOI 10.1214/20-AOAS1384. 

[14] Lo, D. and Park, B. (2016). Modeling the spread of the Zika virus using topological data analysis. *arXiv:1612.03554*. https://arxiv.org/abs/1612.03554

[15] Mullen, A. D., Harris, D., Rock, P., Slavova, S., Talbert, J., and Bumgardner, V. K. C. (2025). Forecasting opioid incidents for rapid actionable data for opioid response in Kentucky.     Drug and Alcohol Dependence 267:111705, DOI 10.1016/j.drugalcdep.2024.111705

[16] Neill, D. B. and Herlands, W. (2018). Machine learning for drug overdose surveillance. Journal of Technology in Human Services, 36(1):8–14,
2018. doi: 10.1080/15228835.2017.1416511

[17] Shumway, R. H. and Stoffer, D. S. (2017). *Time Series Analysis and Its Applications: With R Examples*, 4th edition. Springer.

[18] Soliman, M., Lyubchich, V., and Gel, Y. R. (2020). Ensemble forecasting of the Zika space-time spread with topological data analysis. Environmetrics,
31(7):e2629.

[19] Turner, K. (2023). Representing vineyard modules. *arXiv:2307.06020*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.06020

[20] White, D., Tran, L., and Ma, L. (2021). A statistical analysis of drug seizures and opioid overdose deaths in Ohio from 2014 to 2018. *Journal of Student Research*, 10(1). https://doi.org/10.47611/jsr.v10i1.786

[21] Yang, J., Fang, H., Dhesi, J., Yoon, I. H. R., Bull, J. A., Byrne, H. M., Harrington, H. A., and Grindstaff, G. (2025). Topological classification of tumour-immune interactions and dynamics. Journal of Mathematical
Biology, 91(3):25, doi: 10.1007/s00285-025-02253-6.
